entry.title: Enhancing Performance in SAP Web Applications: Strategies and Best Practices
entry.content:
Understanding the Performance Landscape:

Performance in SAP web applications is a complex issue. It involves several aspects such as response time, data processing efficiency, and user interface fluidity. For our requirement, we engaged Schnell, a UK-based software developer specialising in web application development services . Based on their experience in developing similar applications, they helped us to develop a high-performance, optimised web app with real-time analytics.

They implemented simple strategies like fine-tuning database queries to speed up data retrieval, writing efficient code to minimise network traffic, and using caching techniques to store frequently accessed information locally. They also, focused on optimising the front-end elements like UI design and JavaScript code which greatly enhance the user experience for us. By applying these simple techniques and keeping a close eye on performance metrics, businesses can make their SAP applications more efficient, delivering better results for their users.

Key Strategies for Optimization:

  • Efficient Use of SAP Fiori and UI5: SAP Fiori and SAP UI5 are at the forefront of SAP’s user experience. To optimize performance, it’s essential to minimize the number of round-trips between the client and server, use asynchronous calls judiciously, and leverage caching mechanisms effectively.
  • Optimizing SAP HANA Usage: SAP HANA's in-memory computing capabilities offer significant performance benefits. To fully leverage these, developers should optimize data models, use proper indexing, and ensure efficient query design to minimize data processing times.
  • Code Optimization: In any SAP web application, clean and efficient coding practices are paramount. This includes minimizing unnecessary database calls, optimizing loops and internal tables, and adhering to SAP’s best coding practices.
  • Load Balancing and Scalability: Implementing load balancing techniques and ensuring the application is scalable to handle increased load are vital. This involves balancing the load across servers and optimizing resource allocation.
  • Effective Use of Caching: Caching frequently accessed data reduces the number of database hits, significantly improving response times. Appropriate use of browser and server-side caching can lead to substantial performance gains.
  • Regular Performance Testing: Conducting regular performance testing, including stress and load testing, helps in identifying bottlenecks and areas for improvement. Tools like SAP Load Runner can be instrumental in this process.

Best Practices for SAP Web Application Performance:

  • Monitor Regularly: Continuous monitoring of application performance is essential. Tools like SAP Solution Manager and SAP Fiori Monitor can provide valuable insights into application performance metrics.
  • Optimize Network Performance: Ensure that the network configuration is optimized for the best performance. This includes looking at aspects like bandwidth, latency, and data packet sizes.
  • Responsive Design Considerations: For mobile or responsive web applications, performance optimization also includes UI elements that are lightweight and adaptable to different screen sizes without compromising on performance.
  • User Experience Focus: A performant application is not just about speed. It’s also about how quickly and efficiently users can complete their tasks. This means optimizing navigation flows, search functionalities, and overall application layout.
  • Cloud Integration: When using SAP Cloud Platform, leverage cloud capabilities for better performance. This includes utilizing cloud-based processing power and storage effectively.

It is important to mention that performance optimisation in SAP web applications is a continuous process and should not be viewed as a not a one-time fix. Depending on how the application is used, user feedback, analytics and monitoring, SAP offers may options to fine-tune web applications to ensure they operate efficiently.

entry.title: Re: SAC addin formulas not refreshing
entry.content:
Hello Ioannis,

This is an issue with Microsoft Excel Add-in Analysis ToolPak.

From SAP's side, we have KBA below:
3418085 - The SAC Excel Add-in formulas in locally saved Excel workbooks present a preffix with FUNCRES.XLAM'!_xldudf_

Regards,
Ismael Heinen

entry.title: Maximizing Communication Efficiency with SAP Datasphere Catalog's Business Glossary and Templates
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, effective communication is crucial for data driven decision making, data migration initiatives, analysis, AI/ML scenarios, and reporting success. However, with the increasing complexity of industries and the constant evolution of terminology, it can be challenging for employees, stakeholders, and even customers to understand each other as they interact with data and for supporting data-driven decision with confidence. As organizations grow in size and complexity, ensuring a shared understanding of key terms and concepts becomes increasingly challenging.  

This is where a comprehensive and well-maintained business glossary comes into play here, serving as a vital tool for organizations: 

  • Helping to establish a common language 
  • Acting as a centralized repository of definitions 
  • Fostering clarity and consistency across an organization 
  • Enhancing collaboration 
  • Increase trust and ownership 

In this blog, we will explore the importance of a business glossary, its impact on organizational efficiency and how it can benefit your business. We will also show the importance of having a business glossary template, how a glossary can assist your organization, and the steps on how to create a template glossary in SAP Datasphere catalog. 

The Importance of a Business Glossary 

SAP Datasphere catalog helps organizations manage their data assets. The catalog glossary is a feature within SAP Datasphere that allows users to create and maintain a centralized repository of business terms and definitions. 
 
In business, ambiguity can be a significant obstacle. Without a shared understanding of terminology, employees may interpret information differently, leading to confusion and inefficiency. A business glossary eliminates this ambiguity by providing precise definitions that leave no room for interpretation. A glossary serves as a reference point for defining and standardizing key terms used within an organization. By providing clear and concise definitions, a glossary ensures that everyone is on the same page when discussing critical concepts. This common language promotes effective communication, minimizes misunderstandings, and reduces the risk of misinterpretation.  

 

The catalog glossary provides a centralized location for storing and managing business terms and definitions. This helps to ensure consistency and accuracy across the organization.  

 

A business glossary can foster collaboration by enabling better communication and understanding across different departments or business units. A glossary encourages the sharing of knowledge across the organization. This can increase trust among teams as they can better understand each other's work and align their efforts. Increasing confidence by ensuring that all team members have access to the same information and are working with a common understanding. 

 

In highly regulated industries, compliance with industry-specific standards and regulations is crucial. A business glossary can play a vital role in ensuring compliance by providing clear definitions of terms related to regulations, policies, and procedures. It helps employees understand and adhere to compliance requirements, reducing the risk of non-compliance and associated penalties. A well-maintained business glossary can also serve as evidence of an organization's commitment to regulatory compliance during audits or inspections. 

 

It is also worth noting that there may be times when it is beneficial to create multiple glossaries for specific LoBs (Lines of Businesses), this may occur if there are unique terms and concepts for specific LoBs. Different lines of business often have unique terms, concepts, and definitions that are specific to their operations. A glossary ensures that everyone within the line of business uses the same language and understands these terms in the same way. Having a standard set of definitions for each line of business minimizes the risk of miscommunication or misunderstanding, particularly when dealing with complex or industry-specific terminology. For new employees or stakeholders, a business glossary can serve as a valuable resource to understand the specific terminologies and concepts used in a particular line of business, helping them get up to speed more quickly. 

 

How to Define a Business Glossary Template 

A business glossary template provides a standardized format for creating and maintaining a valuable resource. 

1.  Click Create 
2.  Click Glossary 

3.  Enter Glossary Name 
4.  Enter Description  

 

5.  Click Custom Attributes  
6.  Click Add Custom Attribute 

7.  Enter Attribute Group Name 
8.  Click Add Attribute 

 

 9.  Enter Attribute Name 
10. Enter Description about the Attribute 
11. Select Type (Boolean, Date, Decimal, Integer, Rich Text, or String) 
12. Select Validation Type (Integer: Any Values | List of Values | Range; String: Any Values | List of Values) 

Note: List of Values allows you to choose if you want to allow users to select one value from the list or multiple values from the list 

13. Select Display Column (Left or Right) 
14. Required attribute turn on or off 
15. Click OK 

 

16. Repeat Steps 8-15 as needed 
17. Modify Attributes, as necessary 
18. Modify Layout of Attributes. as necessary 
19. Delete Attribute, as necessary 
20. Move Attribute up or down, as necessary, by selecting attribute and dragging up or down to desire location 

21. Click Save 

 

 

 

Conclusion 

In today's complex business landscape, a business glossary is an invaluable tool for fostering clarity, precision, consistency, and effective communication. By establishing a common language, facilitating knowledge sharing, and supporting compliance, it becomes crucial for any organization to incorporate a business glossary. A business glossary helps bridge the gap between different departments, teams, and stakeholders, fostering collaboration and alignment. Investing time and effort in developing and maintaining a comprehensive business glossary can unlock significant benefits, enabling your business to thrive in an increasingly competitive environment. It helps employees gain clarity on complex topics, enabling them to make informed decisions and take appropriate actions. This clarity extends beyond internal communication and can also enhance your interactions with clients, partners, and suppliers. 

 

I also want to share some additional resources for you around SAP Datasphere:  

  • Access to your free 30 day trial of SAP Datasphere 
  • Join SAP Datasphere community to see what’s new, ask questions, share your feedback, and more 
  • SAP Datasphere product overview  
  • Documentation for SAP Datasphere catalog

entry.title: Consume the SAP Cloud ALM Custom Processes API
entry.content:
Introduction​

SAP Cloud ALM provides public APIs designed to facilitate integration with external systems.

In this blog, I'll highlight the Custom Processes APIs, which facilitate the management of custom solution processes within SAP Cloud ALM.

For developers seeking to configure an SAP Cloud ALM API instance and utilize the Custom Processes APIs via NodeJS code, this blog offers step-by-step guidance. By leveraging these APIs, developers can transfer processes and process diagrams in both SVG and BPMN formats to SAP Cloud ALM.

You can access the documentation for the Custom Processes APIs on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by navigating to the following link.

 

Configure the SAP Cloud ALM API instance​

To expose and consume public APIs from your SAP Cloud ALM tenant, you've to create a service instance and associate scopes to this service instance. These are the different steps:

  1. Go to the SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

  2. Create a service instance from the Service Instance tab.

  3. Enter the instance basic informations: 

  4. Add the service instance scopes by specifying the corresponding JSON format to generate OAuth 2.0 credentials: 

 

 

{
    "xs-security": {
        "xsappname": "CustomProcessesAPI",
        "authorities": [
        "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processauthoring.read",
        "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processauthoring.write",
        "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processauthoring.delete"
        ]
    }
}

 

 
Note: Additionally, you have the option to generate service keys for mTls authentication. Please refer to the provided documentation if you wish to proceed with this method.

  • Use the review step to verify the instance details, then choose Create.

  • Select you service instance and create a service key(only the name is needed): 

  • Select the View Credentials button: 

Note: You are done with the configuration part! The next step requires the clientid, clientsecret, and the url UAA attributes.
 

Consume the Custom Processes API​

The project structure is simple. You need mainly 2 files: package.json and an index.js file:

You have to add 2 dependencies to your package.json file:

  • axios: Promise based HTTP client to perform the rest calls.
  • axios-oauth-client: OAuth 2.0 client utils for axios needed for the authentication.

 

{
  "name": "calm-custom-processes-api-consumer",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A nodejs sample to consume the cloud alm custom processes api.",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node index.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "axios": "^1.6.7",
    "axios-oauth-client": "^2.0.4"
  }
}

 

I've placed the code related to the consumption of the Custom Processes API in an index.js file.

The logic is simple! My aim was to create a Custom Solution Process and include two diagrams: one in BPMN format and the other in SVG.

To test the following code snippet, ensure to replace the placeholders for "user," "password," "tenantAuthUrl," and "tenantBaseUrl" with your respective values.

Note: The code examples don't include error handling, as our primary focus will be on API calls and logic implementation.

 

import axios from "axios";
import oauth from "axios-oauth-client";

const user = "client id"; // The cliendid of SAP Cloud ALM API instance
const password = "client secret"; // The cliendsecret of SAP Cloud ALM API instance
const tenantAuthUrl = "authentication url"; // The url of SAP Cloud ALM API instance
const tenantBaseUrl = "tenant base url"; // The SAP Cloud ALM tenant url, it should be in this format https://{tenant}.{region}.alm.cloud.sap

//Fetch the OAuth token.
//Considering that tokens have a limited validity period, it's worth considering implementing a token cache and refresh mechanism to manage them effectively.
//This is not covered here to keep the code simple. 
const token = await oauth.clientCredentials(
  axios.create({
    baseURL: tenantAuthUrl,
  }),
  "/oauth/token",
  user,
  password
)();

//Create the Axios instance using the tenant base url concatenated to the custom processes api path.
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: tenantBaseUrl + "/api/calm-processauthoring/v1",
});

//Add the OAuth token to all request headers.
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = "Bearer " + token.access_token;
  }
  return config;
});

//Create a Business Process
//A Business Process is needed to create the Custom Solution Process as it's a mandatory field.
//The Business Process name should be unique this is why i'm concatenating the date to the name.
const businessProcess = await api.post("/businessProcesses", {
  name: "API Business Process" + new Date(),
  description: "Business Process Created via API",
});

//Create a Custom Solution Process 
const customSolutionProcess = await api.post("/solutionProcesses", {
  name: "API Custom Solution Process",
  description: "Solution Process Created via API",
  countries: "DE,FR",
  externalId: "PROC1",
  businessProcess: {
    id: businessProcess.data.id,
  },
});

//Get the Solution Process Flows of the created Solution Process
const solutionProcessFlow = await api.get(
  "/solutionProcesses/" +
    customSolutionProcess.data.id +
    "/solutionProcessFlows"
);

//Create a Solution Process Flow Diagram from BPMN content
//For now there is only one Solution Process Flow per Custom Solution Process so we will use it as a container for the diagrams.
const solutionProcessFlowDiagram = await api.post(
  "/solutionProcessFlows/" +
    solutionProcessFlow.data.value[0].id +
    "/solutionProcessFlowDiagrams/bpmn",
  {
    name: "API BPMN Diagram",
    bpmn: '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><definitions xmlns="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL" xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI" xmlns:omgdc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC" xmlns:omgdi="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI" xmlns:signavio="http://www.signavio.com" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" exporter="Signavio Process Editor, http://www.signavio.com" exporterVersion="17.13.1" expressionLanguage="http://www.w3.org/TR/XPath" id="sid-5fdcc601-5000-4ab6-b88e-4433521b62fa" targetNamespace="http://www.signavio.com" typeLanguage="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/2.0/20100501/BPMN20.xsd"><collaboration id="sid-36909f60-615f-4371-b8f3-883e2ffbb3d9"><participant id="sid-C27475A3-EB6E-420E-A50E-512DC50CE5E2" name="Pool" processRef="sid-0E710C9C-2698-4FAC-B253-9706A4E3A461"><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bgcolor" metaValue="#ffffff"/><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/></extensionElements></participant></collaboration><process id="sid-0E710C9C-2698-4FAC-B253-9706A4E3A461" isClosed="false" isExecutable="false" name="Pool" processType="None"><extensionElements/><laneSet id="sid-cfbe2113-6b02-49b3-996d-e6753a57c684"><lane id="sid-8ABC4DD7-9999-497E-BD1A-D9834C7346B6" name="Lane"><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bgcolor" metaValue=""/><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/></extensionElements><flowNodeRef>sid-165FD2A8-553D-464A-A033-A32D33FEF9CF</flowNodeRef><flowNodeRef>sid-57B3B393-8005-45E4-9085-5681FA0F43A9</flowNodeRef><flowNodeRef>sid-5375E623-E07C-4322-B43F-D7EB9AE4BC8D</flowNodeRef></lane></laneSet><startEvent id="sid-165FD2A8-553D-464A-A033-A32D33FEF9CF" name=""><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bgcolor" metaValue="#ffffff"/><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/></extensionElements><outgoing>sid-A0C0A710-028B-4B57-AC2D-091BB8DB7998</outgoing></startEvent><task completionQuantity="1" id="sid-57B3B393-8005-45E4-9085-5681FA0F43A9" isForCompensation="false" name="Task" startQuantity="1"><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bgcolor" metaValue="#ffffcc"/><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="meta-descriptionlongchar" metaValue=""/></extensionElements><incoming>sid-A0C0A710-028B-4B57-AC2D-091BB8DB7998</incoming><outgoing>sid-FE527107-1943-4616-9FB6-5C41EA243301</outgoing></task><endEvent id="sid-5375E623-E07C-4322-B43F-D7EB9AE4BC8D" name=""><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bgcolor" metaValue="#ffffff"/><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/></extensionElements><incoming>sid-FE527107-1943-4616-9FB6-5C41EA243301</incoming></endEvent><sequenceFlow id="sid-A0C0A710-028B-4B57-AC2D-091BB8DB7998" name="" sourceRef="sid-165FD2A8-553D-464A-A033-A32D33FEF9CF" targetRef="sid-57B3B393-8005-45E4-9085-5681FA0F43A9"><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/></extensionElements></sequenceFlow><sequenceFlow id="sid-FE527107-1943-4616-9FB6-5C41EA243301" name="" sourceRef="sid-57B3B393-8005-45E4-9085-5681FA0F43A9" targetRef="sid-5375E623-E07C-4322-B43F-D7EB9AE4BC8D"><extensionElements><signavio:signavioMetaData metaKey="bordercolor" metaValue="#000000"/></extensionElements></sequenceFlow></process><bpmndi:BPMNDiagram id="sid-f98e40b6-c1e4-427f-883d-9639060a0568"><bpmndi:BPMNPlane bpmnElement="sid-36909f60-615f-4371-b8f3-883e2ffbb3d9" id="sid-a3fca2ce-e9c7-4f67-84cb-c63991b958e3"><bpmndi:BPMNShape bpmnElement="sid-C27475A3-EB6E-420E-A50E-512DC50CE5E2" y="192.27142810821533"/></bpmndi:BPMNLabel></bpmndi:BPMNShape><bpmndi:BPMNShape bpmnElement="sid-165FD2A8-553D-464A-A033-A32D33FEF9CF" id="sid-165FD2A8-553D-464A-A033-A32D33FEF9CF_gui"><omgdc:Bounds );

//Create a Solution Process Flow Diagram from SVG content
const solutionProcessFlowDiagram2 = await api.post(
    "/solutionProcessFlows/" +
      solutionProcessFlow.data.value[0].id +
      "/solutionProcessFlowDiagrams",
    {
      name: "API SVG Diagram",
      svg: "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\" standalone=\"no\"?><svg id=\"svgRoot-1\" class=\"svgRoot\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"608\" height=\"258\" xmlns:xlink=\"http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink\" xmlns:galilei=\"http://www.sap.com/2014/galilei\" viewBox=\"0 0 608 258\"><defs><pattern id=\"pagePattern-1\" patternUnits=\"userSpaceOnUse\" x=\"-493\" y=\"-720\" width=\"987\" height=\"1440\"><path d=\"M987 -720 L-493 -720 L-493 1440 L987 1440 L987 -720\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#d9d9d9\" stroke-width=\"1.5\"/></pattern><pattern id=\"gridPattern-1\" patternUnits=\"userSpaceOnUse\" width=\"50\" height=\"50\"><path d=\"M0 10 H50 M0 20 H50 M0 30 H50 M0 40 H50 M10 0 V50 M20 0 V50 M30 0 V50 M40 0 V50\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#CEE0F2\" stroke-width=\"1\"/><path d=\"M50 0 L0 0 L0 50 L50 50 L50 0\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#ADCDED\" stroke-width=\"1.5\"/></pattern><rect id=\"Icons.invisible-icon-1\" fill=\"#000\" opacity=\"0\" x=\"0\" y=\"0\" width=\"0\" height=\"0\"/><g id=\"Bpmn.CompensationIcon-1\" focusable=\"false\"><path class=\"sapGalileiBpmnSubProcessIconStroke\" stroke=\"black\" stroke-width=\"1\" fill=\"none\" d=\"M0,6 L5.5,0 L5.5,12 Z\"/><path class=\"sapGalileiBpmnSubProcessIconStroke\" stroke=\"black\" stroke-width=\"1\" fill=\"none\" d=\"M6.5,6 L12,0 L12,12 Z\"/></g><marker id=\"Arrows.FilledEnd-428gb0-1\" class=\"ArrowsFilledEnd\" markerUnits=\"userSpaceOnUse\" refX=\"10.5\" refY=\"5\" markerWidth=\"14\" markerHeight=\"10\" orient=\"auto\"><path stroke=\"none\" fill=\"#428EB0\" d=\"M 0 0 L 14 5 L 0 10 z\"/></marker></defs><g id=\"layersGroup\"><g id=\"svgMainShapeLayer-1\" class=\"svgShapeLayer\" cursor=\"default\" transform=\"translate(-206,-76)\"><g id=\"ecc68382-907f-47e0-8183-b4859be1e527\" class=\"sapGalileiSymbolNode\" focusable=\"true\" sap-automation=\"Pool/Pool\" transform=\"translate(210,80)\" style=\"user-select: none; -webkit-user-drag: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0); touch-action: none;\"><title>End Event</title><g focusable=\"false\"><circle class=\"endEvent\" stroke=\"#CC2424\" stroke-width=\"3\" fill=\"#FFE6E5\" cx=\"14\" cy=\"14\" r=\"14\"/><use style=\"pointer-events: none;\" x=\"0\" y=\"0\" width=\"0\" height=\"0\" xmlns:ns2=\"http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink\" ns2:href=\"#Icons.invisible-icon-1\"/></g></g><g id=\"4b3cc168-8834-4210-815c-c941864dd49e\" class=\"sapGalileiSymbolNode\" focusable=\"true\" sap-automation=\"StartEvent\" transform=\"translate(400,190)\" style=\"user-select: none; -webkit-user-drag: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0); touch-action: none;\"><title>Start Event</title><g focusable=\"false\"><circle class=\"startEvent\" stroke=\"#288000\" stroke-width=\"1\" fill=\"#ECFDDC\" display=\"inline\" cx=\"15\" cy=\"15\" r=\"15\"/><use style=\"pointer-events: none;\" x=\"0\" y=\"0\" width=\"0\" height=\"0\" xmlns:ns3=\"http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink\" ns3:href=\"#Icons.invisible-icon-1\"/></g></g></g></g></svg>"
    }
  );

 

 

Result​

Navigate to the SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring app and look for the Custom Solution Process you have created. You should see your diagrams:

 

Conclusion​

In the blog, I have provided a detailed guide on configuring an SAP Cloud ALM API instance with the Custom Process Authoring scopes. Additionally, I've included straightforward code examples to demonstrate how to consume this API. You can also clone the Github Repository and follow the readme instructions.

For more context about the Import of BPMN models in SAP Cloud ALM, please refer to this blog.

If you want to know more about SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring capabilities, please refer to the Walkthrough SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring blog.

entry.title: Highlights from DSAG's SAP ALM Thementage Stuttgart 2024 and Technology Days Hamburg 2024
entry.content:
The DSAG ALM Thementage Stuttgart 2024 and the DSAG Technology Days Hamburg 2024 are vibrant hubs of innovation and collaboration, bringing together professionals across diverse industries. With a lineup of esteemed speakers and thought leaders, these events offer a dynamic platform to explore the cutting-edge trends and transformative practices reshaping the landscape of SAP Application Lifecycle Management (ALM).

 

DSAG ALM Thementage Stuttgart 2024:

The sold-out event in Stuttgart attracted 300 attendees and unfolded over two days, featuring content-rich sessions across three rooms. These sessions covered a spectrum of ALM topics, facilitating intimate discussions among customers, partners, and SAP experts. Notable highlights included participants gaining insight into the future of SAP ALM, along with updates on the latest roadmaps and upcoming features.

The event kicked off with an opening keynote, "Energize your ALM Transformation!" delivered by Marc Thier from SAP and Sebastian Westphal from Deutsche Bahn. Marc Thier shared his vision for the evolution of ALM, ensuring attendees were well-equipped to adapt and leverage these advancements. Sebastian Westphal shared real-life experiences of transformation at Deutsche Bahn, enlightening participants with valuable insights.

A standout moment was the guest keynote by Patrick Ratheiser, CEO of Leftshift One, who explored the potential of GenAI in the SAP realm. His insights shed light on integrating AI technologies in ALM processes and across the entire SAP ecosystems, promising enhanced efficiency and value generation.

The event also featured a Meet the Expert area, providing attendees with a platform to engage with experts from SAP, Signavio, LeanIX, Tricentis, and Leftshift One, fostering knowledge exchange and networking opportunities. The DSAG ALM Thementage Stuttgart 2024 offered a holistic view of the future of SAP ALM, addressing challenges and innovations like artificial intelligence and empowering companies to optimize processes and drive digital transformation.

 

DSAG Technology Days Hamburg 2024:

Building on the success of its predecessor, the DSAG Technology Days in Hamburg attracted over 3000 participants, surpassing the venue's capacity and underscoring the event's significance. The four sessions of the working group Application Lifecycle Management provided insights into SAP Cloud ALM, transition strategies from SAP Solution Manager, and the Business Transformation Center.

The first sessions kicked off with compelling presentations delivered by SAP luminaries Tim Steuer, Benjamin Schneider, and Thorsten Spihlmann, focused on the "SAP ALM Overview and Roadmap," offering insights into SAP Cloud ALM, the transition from SAP Solution Manager and the Business Transformation Center. The second session, led by Janko Budzisch, delved into the operations of customer landscapes with SAP Cloud ALM, focusing on "Monitoring SAP BTP with SAP Cloud ALM and OpenTelemetry." This session provided attendees with a deeper understanding of the capabilities and benefits of SAP Cloud ALM.

In addition to these informative sessions, the event featured two customer experience sessions. The first, presented by Hapag-Lloyd AG, showcased "A modern Toolchain as baseline for the SAP S/4HANA Journey of the Hapag-Lloyd AG". The second session, presented by Migros-Genossenschafts-Bund, highlighted "Optimization of the ALM toolchain for the SAP S/4HANA transformation@ Migros".

The DSAG Technology Days Hamburg 2024 served as a platform for professionals to expand their knowledge and networks within the SAP ALM community. Participants were left equipped with actionable insights and renewed inspiration to harness the limitless potential of SAP ALM in the digital landscape.

 

Feedback:

Attendees provided overwhelmingly positive feedback, expressing appreciation for the opportunity to engage and learn from the events. The success of these gatherings reflects the commitment of the SAP community to advancing innovation and excellence in ALM.

Some of the received feedback includes:

Great thing! We look forward to working with you to further expand the #cloudalm topic! #tricentis" - Stefanie Lanzer

"All in all, very successful. It was a very exciting two days." - Lukas Lütjann from CONET

''Dear ALM team. The meeting was well organized, and the presentations very well orchestrated 🙂 Thanks to all." - Peter Flury, Bundesamt für Informatik und Telekommunikation BIT

"I was also very happy to be there again! The exchange, information, and networking are priceless!" - Fabian Bischoff from the WITTENSTEIN group

"I'm not surprised at all, Sven Präßler, Benjamin Schneider, Tim Steuer. These are also massively interesting topics that the three of you present." - ACHIM T.

 

Menti Survey Highlights:

The survey feedback paints a promising picture of cloud adoption trends. Notably, 10% of companies have transitioned to the Cloud, while 25% have solidified robust cloud strategies, demonstrating proactive strides toward digital transformation. A significant majority, comprising 43% of companies, have embraced hybrid infrastructure models, seamlessly integrating on-premise solutions with cloud services.

From the customer perspective, the survey reveals an encouraging trend in SAP Cloud ALM adoption. Among attendees, 14% already leverage SAP Cloud ALM, while an impressive 33% plan to transition or implement it by 2027. This collective effort underscores a profound shift toward modernization and innovation. The traditional on-premises footprint is yielding to hybrid solutions and cloud integration, reflecting strategic alignment among companies.

This alignment signifies that businesses are embracing the Cloud and strategically leveraging it to stay competitive and innovative in an ever-evolving digital landscape. It reflects a strategic commitment to harnessing the full potential of cloud technology for operational excellence and future growth."

 

Future Outlook:

Looking ahead, the SAP ALM community eagerly anticipates forthcoming events fostering growth, connection, and discovery. Here's a preview of what to expect at upcoming gatherings -

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/alm-events

Take action now and mark your calendars for the latest upcoming SAP ALM events of the year.

ALM Roadshows:

  • March 19: Mumbai, India
  • March 20: Hyderabad, India

Hurry and register here: ALM Roadshows (sap.com)

Community Calls:

  • March 20: SAP Community Call for ALM
  • March 26: SAP Community Call for ALM - APJ Edition

Join us at the community calls: SAP ALM Community Calls

 ALM Summits:

  • July 23-25: SAP ALM Summit APJ, India
  • September 24-27: SAP ALM Summit EMEA, Germany

Register to secure your spot: SAP ALM Summits

Stay tuned for more information.

 

DSAG ALM Thementage Stuttgart 2024 and DSAG Technology Days Hamburg 2024 stand as testimonials to the collaborative spirit and dedication of the SAP community to drive progress and innovation in Application Lifecycle Management.

Your voice shapes the future of collective knowledge. We welcome your suggestions for future events, topics and ways to improve our content.

entry.title: Navigate Your Way to SAP PaPM Universal Model
entry.content:
Hello! How was your first encounter with SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud - Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM) via this blogpost Unveiling the new functionality in 2024 of SAP PAPM Cloud: Welcome to Universal Model! Is It interesting?

To make you even more comfortable in using the newest functionality of SAP PaPM Cloud, keep reading this blogpost as this will serve as your initial guide to navigate through the user-friendly features of Universal Model. Before we begin the tour, I would like to prepare you of what to expect from this blogpost, such as:

(A) Overview of Your Home Screen 
(B) How to Customize Your Home Screen 
(C) How to Access Applications in Universal Model 
(D) Short Introduction to SAP Companion 

Are you ready? If yes, then let’s start our tour! 😊

Note
This document is not a substitute of the SAP Help Portal Documentation. To learn more about the detailed functionalities of Universal Model, see SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud: Universal Model SAP Help Portal Documentation.

(A) Overview of Your Home Screen 

The Home screen of Universal Model is designed to provide swift and effortless access to your most frequently used tools and applications.

Here is a quick overview of the different sections featured on your Home screen:

  • Shell bar: Helps you navigate across different applications of Universal Model. To know more about these attributes, see image and table below: 

  • Application Group: Displays the list of applications for business users in Universal Model.
  • Application Tiles: Displays the preset application tiles available and categorized into different groups.

(B) How to Customize Your Home Screen 

Now that we are bit familiar with what we see on our Home screen, let’s now go into how we can customize it for a more personalized, tailored fitting look for you.

By choosing your initials on the top right side of the shell bar, the system will lead you to your profile.

The user profile displays various options to personalize your Universal Model application, allowing you to customize how you view and organize your workspace.

To learn more about each attribute, see the table below for reference:

(C) How to Access Applications in Universal Model

Now that we have customized our application, let’s find out how we can access the applications that are most useful for us.

There are three ways on how to access an application in Universal Model.
You may choose one from the following options:

 Via Home screen:

  1. Go to the Home screen.
  2. Locate the application icon or name of the application.
  3. Choose the appropriate tile to open the application.

Via Search bar:

  1. Choose the Search bar.
  2. Type the name of the application.
  3. Select the relevant result from the dropdown menu.

Via All Apps menu:

  1. Go to the Home screen.
  2. Expand the Navigation Menu.
  3. Choose the relevant catalog, then choose the application.

(D) Short Introduction to SAP Companion

Needing some assistance in the system? We have exciting news for you! Not only that Universal Model was created with a user-friendly interface, we have also embedded a companion just for you. 😊

Introducing…
SAP Companion – a feature within the Universal Model application. It consists of (i) Static Help and (ii) Guided Tour that will help you work in the system.

To access these features, choose Open Help on your shell bar on any page. The system opens it in a side panel, and will automatically set the static help active on your screen.

Note
SAP Companion is available only in English language.

(i) Static Help
By choosing a specific help topic on the right panel of your screen, the system will pop up the long description of the topic chosen. 

For example:

(ii) Guided Tour
This is a help embedded in some of Universal Model’s application that will serve as your “friend” within the system. It will provide a step-by-step guide that will help you configure your way through an application.

To access this feature, choose Guided Tour on the right side panel of your SAP Companion. The system lists all guided tours available within that specific application.

Note
Guided tours are available only on selected applications and will continue to grow.

For example:
How to create an environment.

  1. Choose Guided Tour on the right side panel of your SAP Companion.
  2. Choose TOUR_2 Create Environment from the list.
  3. In this case, we will create our first environment. First, choose Create.
  4. Then, select “Environment” from the dropdown list. Then choose Next in the Guided Tour popup.
  5. Choose “Continue”.
  6. You will be redirected to the main configuration screen where you will be asked to fill in the mandatory fields such as “Environment” and “Description”, and non-mandatory fields such as “Parent”, “Reader Team”, “Writer Team”, and so on. In this example, I only fill in these two mandatory fields. Choose Next in the Guided Tour popup.

    Tip
    Scroll up and down the help popup window to find the description for each field.
  7. Choose “Create”.
  8. The system will prompt Object created.

And that’s it… You have just created your first environment in Universal Model! 😊
Thank you so much for “touring” with me!

Happy Modeling! 😊

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition for Service | 2023 FPS1 Product Update
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition for Service | 2023 FPS1 release:

  • Service Price Items
  • Execution order items with multiple connected maintenance orders
  • Project integration for Service with Advanced Execution
  • Event-based revenue recognition for ad-hoc billing plan of service orders with advanced execution
  • Enhancements in Resource-related Billing for Execution Order items

Service Price Items

Using Service Price items, we are now able to support price increases or changes to the service scope in service quotations and service orders. Service price items are always sub-items below another service quotation or order item. These price items are not relevant for confirmation and are billed with fixed-price (order-based billing). The additional revenue is booked on the same Accounting Object as the main item. This allows consolidated financial reporting and revenue recognition across service items and service price adjustment items.

Service price items provide additional commercial flexibility while keeping consistent financial reporting.

Picture: Service Price Items

 

Execution order items with multiple  connected maintenance orders

As of release 2023 FPS1, it is now possible to connect multiple maintenance orders to one single (commercial) execution order item in the service order. This allows to split detailed planning and execution of services across multiple maintenance orders, while providing consolidated financial reporting of revenue and costs at the level of the service order item (by re-using a single accounting object).

A first maintenance order is created when an Execution Order item in the Service Order is released. Afterwards, additional maintenance orders can be created in 3 possible ways:

  • Create new maintenance order as a copy of the first maintenance order
  • Create new maintenance order as a follow-up of the first maintenance order
  • Create new maintenance order as a sub-order

This new capability in Service with Advanced Execution delivers more detailed planning of complex, long-running service jobs by splitting planning and execution across multiple maintenance orders. It allows financial reporting and revenue recognition at the level of one single execution order item in the Service order.

Picture: Execution Order item with multiple maintenance orders

 

Picture: Execution Order item with multiple maintenance orders

 

Project integration for Service with Advanced Execution

The maintenance orders created out of the Service with Advanced Execution process are called “Billable Maintenance Orders”. We have now enabled WBS assignment in billable maintenance orders. This WBS assignment makes the costs of the maintenance order visible in the Project reporting.

Picture: Billable maintenance order in Project reporting

 

Event-based revenue recognition for ad-hoc billing plan of service orders with advanced execution

The determination of a revenue recognition key for execution order items with ad-hoc billing plan has been enabled in customizing.

Picture: Revenue Recognition Key derivation

 

Enhancements in Resource-related Billing for Execution Order items

Resource-related billing has been enhanced with these features:

  • Copy of Terms of payment from Service Order to Debit Memo Request (DMR)
  • Copy of External reference from Service Order to Debit Memo Request (DMR)
  • Copy of Business partners of execution order item to Debit Memo Request (DMR)
  • Alternate tax classification (from Service Order Header level)
  • Copy of SEPA mandate reference from Service Order to Debit Memo Request (DMR)
  • Copy of Contract Account reference (FICA active) from Service Order to Debit Memo Request (DMR)
  • Expenses captured on Execution Order item accounting object can be included in Resource-related billing

 

Explore the SAP S/4HANA Service Community, a centralized hub for resource collection. Uncover the comprehensive learning materials available on this platform.

I'm a technical guy in charge of BTP.  I've been reading documentation about how to run the "installation/deploy" of SAP subscription billing solution using the booster or following the manual steps (PRD).  Here my doubt is about the environments, this solution must be deploy one per environments? Thanks a mill.

entry.title: Merging Spaces and Pages
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2302 SAP has already shipped predefined spaces and pages that merged with each other on the SAP Fiori launchpad.

Merging is now also available for customers with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, SAP S/4HANA 2023 Private Edition FPS01 and SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

Merging gives you much more flexibility for your spaces and pages.

How can merging help?

  • Merging can help to avoid duplication of apps, spaces, or pages for the user.
  • In addition, merging can be used to enrich the content of spaces and pages. Here are some examples:
    • Extend a space by one or multiple additional pages.
    • Extend a page by the sections and apps of another page. You can use merging to enhance custom spaces, but you can also use merging to combine predefined spaces and pages from SAP with your custom spaces and pages.
  • Merging can happen between two or more spaces and/or two or more pages.

Defining and resolving

Merging is defined at design time by administrators. At runtime the actual merging takes place for the individual business user.

Want to dig into more details of where and how?
…then, please continue reading below.

Administrators at design time

  • Administrators define which spaces or pages should be merged.
  • The General Data section of the apps Manage Launchpad Spaces and Manage Launchpad Pages respectively have been enhanced by the Merge ID.  
  • The Merge ID is used to define which spaces or pages are to be merged.
    • It is possible to merge two or more spaces or two or more pages respectively.
    • A Merge ID can be a random name, or it can also be the ID of a space or a page with which to be merged. The ID of that space or page will then become the merge ID for the spaces or pages that are to be merged.
    • It is good practice to start your own Merge IDs with ‘Z’. You should not use the prefix ‘SAP’ in the IDs for your own merge processes. This could lead to unwanted merging effects.
  • In the same General Data section, we can find the sort priority. The lower the value is, the more important the space turns. This means, spaces with lower sort priority, i.e. spaces that appear more to the left in the space menu, take the lead.

Let’s go through some more tangible examples now…

Starting point for our 3 examples: 

The three examples that we describe next, are based on spaces a user sees in the own space menu, and that are derived from the assigned business roles. In our specific case without merging, we have the following situation: the two spaces Customer Returns and Billing are shown in the menu with their corresponding content, in our example one page per space:

Graph: User sees one space for Customer Returns and one Space for Billing before any merge is performed.

Example 1: Merging Spaces

Since the tasks in the two spaces above are somehow related it is preferable to bring the content closer together in a more concise way for the business user, so that only one space appears on the menu including the pages of each original space. To achieve this, we can merge the two spaces together.

How does the administrator define this?

From the General Data section of the Manage Launchpad Spaces app:
The administrator enters the same Merge ID into each of the spaces to be merged and considers or adapts the sort priority accordingly.
Please note this differs in case of merging a custom space with a predefined space: Predefined spaces cannot be changed, so the predefined space has to stay as it is. In this case, you only enter the merge ID into the custom space and you have to use the existing merge ID of the predefined space as merge ID. If the merge ID of the predefined space is empty, use its space ID.

Graph: Both spaces are assigned the same Merge ID. In addition, the space for Billing shows has a lower value of sort priority and thus, takes the lead and determines the title of the merged space as well as the sequence of how the single pages will be merged.

What does the user see?

At runtime the user sees one space with the title Billing (from the leading Space) with the two pages Billing Processes and Customer Returns in this sequence due to the sort priority value mentioned before.  

Graph: The graph shows the result the user sees after merging at runtime. Since the Billing space has the lower sort priority value, it determines the title of the merged space and the sequence of the pages (1st Billing, 2nd Customer Returns).

Example 2: Merging Pages

In this example we aim to combine two pages, so that the sections of each of the original pages appear together merged on one page. This can be done, for example to bring together apps that are related to a specific process or enrich the process with a relevant flavor.

Please note, if you merge pages only on page level, the resulting merged page at runtime will appear in the specific merged way (with merged title and merged sequence of sections and apps) in all spaces where the pages that are merged are assigned to.

How does the administrator define this?

From the General Data section of the Manage Launchpad Pages app:

The administrator enters the same Merge ID into each of the pages to be merged. The sequence of the page sections is determined by the sort priority of the space a page is assigned to.

Please note: this differs in case of merging a custom page with a predefined page: Predefined pages cannot be changed, so the predefined space has to stay as it is. In this case, you only enter the merge ID into the custom page and you have to use the existing merge ID of the predefined page as merge ID. If the merge ID of the predefined page is empty, use its page ID.

Sections from different pages which have the same title will automatically be merged together as one section on the target page. Please keep in mind that if you specifically want to merge two dedicated sections with each other, that their translations in the target language(s) need to be the same.

Graph: Both Pages are assigned the same merge ID.

What does the user see?

At runtime the user sees one merged page with the sequence of sections and apps that is defined by the sorting priority of the corresponding spaces the original pages belong to.
Please note: At runtime, this merged page will replace the original pages in all spaces that contain one or more of the original pages.
That means that the user will only see the merged page and not the original one(s).

A note on Personalization: The merge ID is used as ID of the merged page and user personalization is stored for this ID. If the merge ID is identical to one of the page IDs, user personalization for that page is visible for the pages with this ID. Otherwise the personalization for the original pages is not taken into account in the merged page.

Graph: At runtime the merged page appears in all spaces where the original pages belong to, replacing them.

Example 3: Combine Merging of Spaces and Pages

In this example we aim to combine all in one. That means we aim to merge content of pages and of spaces at the same time. Our result is a single page within a single space with content merged accordingly.

How does the administrator define this?

From the General Data section of the Manage Launchpad Spaces app:
The administrator enters the merge ID in all of the spaces as described in example 1 and then performs the merging of pages as shown in example 2. All spaces have one common merge ID and all pages have one common merge ID. (We recommend that the merge ID for the spaces are different from the merge ID for the pages to avoid confusion.)

In the merged spaces and pages, the sort priorities of the spaces determine the title of the merged space and the sequence of pages and page sections.

You can check the screenshots presented above for the administrator in examples 1 and 2.

What does the user see?

At runtime the user sees one merged space with one merged page. The merged page shows the sequence of sections and apps that is defined by the sorting priority of the corresponding spaces the original pages belong to.

Graph:  At runtime one space appears with one merged page

Important Note:

Our examples showed merging between custom spaces and custom pages. However, you can also merge your custom spaces with predefined spaces.

Also, we depicted the examples by merging two entities: two spaces and / or two pages.
However, you can also merge more than two. This applies again to the merging between spaces only, pages only, or spaces and pages.

Stay tuned for more!

Further Documentation:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Merging Spaces | SAP Help Portal

  • SAP S/4HANA Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA: Merging Spaces | SAP Help Portal

Further Blogs:

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition:

  • Getting Familiar with Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4H... - SAP Community
  • Deactivation of Groups in the SAP Fiori Launchpad ... - SAP Community
  • How to Efficiently Switch from Groups to Spaces an... - SAP Community

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition / SAP S/4HANA:

Info Package: Making the Move from Launchpad Group... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing environments (DEV, QAS, PRD)
entry.content: When it comes to deploying SAP solutions, including subscription billing solutions, it's common practice to have separate environments for development (DEV), quality assurance (QAS), and production (PRD). This separation helps ensure proper testing, validation, and stability before deploying changes to the production environment. Having separate environments helps maintain a controlled and predictable deployment process, reduces the risk of introducing errors or issues into production, and provides a clear separation of concerns between development, testing, and production activities. Additionally, you may find community forums, SAP support resources, or consulting services helpful for specific deployment scenarios or challenges.

entry.title: Re: "Team overview"
entry.content: Verify that the managers have been assigned the correct user roles with appropriate permissions to access performance reviews. Ensure that they have been assigned the necessary roles at the appropriate organizational level to view reviews for their indirect reports. Check if there are any security settings or restrictions in place that may be preventing managers from accessing performance reviews. This includes both system-level security settings as well as any custom security configurations that may have been implemented. Try accessing performance reviews for indirect reports using test user accounts that have been assigned the same roles and permissions as the managers experiencing the issue. This can help determine if the problem is widespread or specific to certain users.

entry.title: Re: Redirect files that can be uploaded by Commerce custo...
entry.content:
Dear @joaocarreira,

In anwer to your query, whenever one split a large redirect file into multiple ones and attempt to upload them to Cloud Portal, they would be indeed overriding each other. This is for it was designed to support only a single redirect file.

In order to resolve this issue you could follow the resolution described in KBA 3101008, which is either:

  • Simplify your redirect set rules so they are smaller than the threshold.
  • Try to use RedirectMatch instead, or.
  • Use a CDN and apply the redirect rules on a CDN level.
  • Implement via JavaScript.

Also worth mentioning KBA 3120098 - Questions about Redirect Sets in SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud.

 

Hope you'll find this anwer useful.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Dynamically sum up...
entry.content: To achieve the flexibility you're seeking in your calculation, where you want to dynamically sum up the last 12 months from the current month, you can use date functions and relative references. 1.Start by obtaining the current month and year. You can use date functions available in your tool or programming language to do this. 2.Subtract 12 months from the current month to determine the starting month of the 12-month period. 3.Use the current month as the end date of your period. 4.Sum up the values within the specified date range. Start_Date = Current_Date - 12 Months End_Date = Current_Date Sum(Values) Where Date >= Start_Date and Date <= End_Date SELECT SUM(Value) FROM Table WHERE Date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -12, CURRENT_DATE) AND Date <= CURRENT_DATE

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Dynamically sum up...
entry.content:
@guenay 

Keep your aggregate and write to as it is but change your memberset as below

MEMBERSET [d/ZACCOUNT_TEST]="SALARY"
MEMBERSET [d/Date]= PREVIOUS(12,"MONTH",TODAY()) TO PREVIOUS(1,"MONTH",TODAY())

DATA([d/ZACCOUNT_TEST]="SALARYPREV12",[d/Date]="202401")=RESULTLOOKUP()

 

I am just writing the sum to 202401. This will be dynamic based on current date and hence current month.

 

 

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Build Apps + Google Vertex AI API ( Gemini Pro ) + CAP - Get Gemini API inside SAP Build Apps..
entry.content:
As we are witnessing one of the exciting time in the field of AI and lots of experiments and evolutions are still happening , the things we never thought of are coming into reality hence the only option for us to Keep adopting these beautiful break through in tech. and Keep supporting Supporting community and Humanity by every means.

Intended Audience:

Someone who wants to start exploring these APIs and willing to integrate/consume them inside SAP BTP Box then this is your article to read.

Hi All ,

As i have stated above this article is written to give you information about how can we leverage google vertex AI APIs into SAP Build Apps smoothly by adding the CAP Layer in middle to establish communication between them.

Lets start enabling and Testing Vertex API on google cloud.

  • Login into your GCP account and create a project name it "callGeminiAPI" or something.
  • Select your project and now enable Vertex AI APIs - Choose enable and go to Vertex AI Dashboard.

Once you enabled these API , you can see this screen and try out few prompts before we switch to consumption of these APIs/Models.

Lets try some prompt for converting an email request data into particular JSON format using this model , you can try prompts according to your need/testing.

Disclaimer: I am not promoting any AI Product , I am just exploring these APIs and sharing the learning and possibilities of consumption/integration with them.

 

Prompt : I uploaded the above image and added a simple text "What is this image about ?" and got a satisfactory answer from API.

 

I think we are good here , i am not gonna add other prompts here , i shall request you to try out few more prompts and check accuracy and results carefully before deciding the usage of these APIs.

Next Part: How to consume these APIs in SAP CAP.

  • Create a simple CAP Application using BAS/VSCode , I prefer VSCode hence going with that
  • Install required libraries related to Vertex AI APIs.
  • Get your authentication file downloaded from GCP Account ( Most important ) to Authorize the API Calls

Lets do this. let download the service key from your service Account in GCP.

Navigate to your project -> To Service Account and Click create/ Add new Key download JSON and Save it somewhere on your computer.

Go to your Vertex AI API Prompt and navigate to Code section and get the Code Choose NodeJS code as we are going to use exactly the same code to call these APIs from CAP.

Note: I am not going to add anything related to CAP Application , this will be a simple Application with one entity called GEMINI prompt as an input and response in output and create a corresponding handler to handle the incoming request from UI.

Once you click on Get Code , you will get the NodeJS Code related to this API , install required libraries and test the integration.

if you are good with CAP Layer integration with Vertex AI then you will be able to test these APIs from POSTMAN Successfully , Make sure to Authenticate request successfully before model call otherwise it is gonna give you some error related to unauthorized.

Lets test these APIs from POSTMAN now.

So we have completed CAP Integration , Awesome lets build and deploy this API into your BTP Box.

MBT Build + CF Deploy _mtar -> Done ?

Final Part: Integrate CAP APIs into your SAP Build App.

Go to your BTP Cockpit and Open SAP Build Apps 

Choose create an Application and select Mobile App ( aka Appgyver )

Create a Simple Application with one input one button and an output field ( A Chatbot interface ).

Integration with CAP APIs.

Go to Data Section and choose Rest Based Direct Integration

  1. Get Oauth token for CAP API Call
  2. Call Gemini API using this token 

These APIs will be integrated at the button click event to complete the call to backend.

Lets Test this Application end-2-end.

 

At last : Don't forget to share feedback/suggestions if any. 

#KeepLearning#KeepSharing

Thanks - Shivam

entry.title: Re: Content Catalog Sync is taking too long or much time
entry.content:
There's no simple answer for this question. There are several steps in order to improve performance of Catalog Synchronization. Here are few tips:

  1. If SAP Commerce Cloud is where your implementation is hosted, a database analysis is necessary to find the expensive queries. If locally or on premise, JDBC logs can help.

  2. Increasing the number of threads ( catalog.sync.workers ) in the service configuration or local.properties. On Cloud default value of this property is #cores, which calculates number of threads per CPU core.

  3. Reduction of Root Types, i.e the ones coming from an external system. It should also be considered whether there is a need to synchronize product images and other media. There can be a really huge number of medias in the catalog and the media can have a very big impact on synchronization performance. In many cases the synchronization of media can just be avoided.

  4. Ordering of root types. In case there are custom catalog item types, the ordering needs to be adapted according to the data model.

  5. Disabling attributes. If some attributes are not needed in the Online catalog version then these can be removed from the synchronization rule.

  6. Setting Sync languages. The languages of the catalog version should be set to ensure that only the localized values are copied for the supported languages.

  7. Queries with missing indexes

  8. You have some relations where one side of the relations has a high cardinality. For example I have seem one one project we have a category with 200K products. You can disable one side of the relation if this is the case (e.g. disable category.products)

credit: https://community.sap.com/t5/customer-relationship-management-q-a/content-catalog-sync-is-taking-too-long-or-much-time/qaq-p/12808042

entry.title: How do I best organize large Data Services jobs for reliability?
entry.content:
We have about 2000 data flows currently organized in about 500 work flows and 50 jobs.  The jobs are run using ActiveBatch, an enterprise scheduling tool.  Although some of the objects are sequential due to required prerequisites, most of them are simply designed to spread out and organize the load.

Currently some of the largest tables that take hours to load have their own job.  Other jobs are small and can be rerun easily.  But some jobs have a large number of data flows and/or large tables and take a long time to run.  When these have an error, it's complicated, as we don't always have time to rerun the job.  So I'm looking for ways to reorganize things to recover more easily; I need to get as much data as possible loaded as fast as possible, without rerunning more than is necessary and with the ability to move past unresolvable errors.

There are two main scenarios: (1) transient errors such as memory, in which case the job can usually be rerun successfully, and (2) data issues, in which case the job would fail again.  In the ideal world we could just use recovery mode, but it needs an option to either skip or rerun the failed objects.  And we have not been able to get it to work with ActiveBatch anyway.

For scenario 2, in most cases we would rather have the job skip over the bad data and continue and load everything possible.  It seems to do this when the table is loaded in bulk mode, but that is not an option with template tables.  I tried using overflow files but that didn't help.  There ought to be a setting for jobs/workflows to continue in the case of failure.  There are options like like try/catch blocks and status tables, but that would take a lot of work.

One option is to put more data flows into a single workflow and letting the max number of processes regulate it.  Since a job won't fail until the workflow is complete that the failed data flow is in, this would allow everything to complete except the one that failed.  This would be good for scenario 2 but bad for scenario 1 as we'd have to rerun everything.  I am dumbfounded that DS doesn't have a setting to regular the number of processes at the server level though.  

The ideal way would be to put everything into its own job and manage it in ActiveBatch, but that is a different group and they wouldn't be too happy about it.  But I think I probably need to break things up into more jobs one way or another.

So I'm wondering what your strategies are.  Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Smartedit 2011 paragraph editor not saving
entry.content:
Hey There!

The "Style Classes" field in SmartEdit is used with Composable Storefront (formerly known as Spartacus) which will store style class information, so the storefront can apply render logic based on it.

You are probably facing this unwanted behavior with the HTML in paragraph component due to before 1905.19, CKEditor.config.allowedContent is always true in CKEditor config which enables the inline style. For security reason there was introduced new property "cms.components.allowUnsafeJavaScript" in SmartEdit. The property is set to "false" by default, requiring to set it to "true" to be able to inject HTML scripts in paragraph components.

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Commerce Cloud Product Support - Brasil

entry.title: Re: Why many components are blacklisted in smartedit?
entry.content:
Hey There!

As per SmartEdit FAQ - Components, the components are blocked because new storefronts don't support them by default. If your storefront implementation or version supports a blocked component, you can remove it from the attribute blocklist and then edit them using SmartEdit.

Hope this helps answering your doubt!

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Commerce Cloud Product Support - Brasil

entry.title: Re: About Workarea Folder Structure in SAP Enable Now for Simulations & In-App Content
entry.content:
Hi Angad,

No, you should not let the content under the Unsorted folder grow; this will ultimately lead to performance issues with your Enable Now system.  It's good that you are structuring the content under the Content folder but that entire structure should be moved out and placed into the actual content structure.

You are unlikely to use a similar structure for in-app help, assuming that you mean SAP Companion, because the vast majority of your content creation and management occurs through the SAP Companion interface.  I would move all SAP Companion content objects, such as Guided Tours and Context Help content, out of the Unsorted folder and place it into a content structure but you don't need to worry about the Creation vs Published level for this type of content.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF Index table always stores first occurrence of...
entry.content:
The error message "Use a different index table for single selection; 010 field" indicates you have wrongly maintained the same table in field Message Idx Table (/AIF/CUST => Define Namespace-Specific Features => Define Interface-Specific Features) as the concerned multiple index table.

You should either leave it empty (then the default /AIF/STD_IDX_TBL will be used) or maintain your own single-index table in field Message Idx Table (/AIF/CUST => Define Namespace-Specific Features => Define Interface-Specific Features).

entry.title: Intermittent CSS(Cascading Style Sheet) not applied to BTP standalone AppRouter when redirecting 403
entry.content:
Dear,

While I'm testing AppRouter with Spring-boot Java application in BTP, I met an issue as below and so please check it.

entry.title: Re: Enable SAP Companion in SAP Concur - SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Emmanuel,

Firstly, SAP Companion in Concur should look exactly the same as SAP Companion in Fiori because it's the same code that provides the SAP Companion interface and functionality.

SAP Companion must be turned on in Concur; individual customers must request that this done. 

I have seen Concur systems with the menu that you mention but it is another DAP, not SAP Companion.  Did you request that SAP Companion be turned on by generating the link to your SAP Companion configuration in Manager and adding it to a Concur support ticket with the type User Assistant / Tools.  Make sure that you include the URL of your SAP Companion configuration in the ticket and list the Concur systems(s) that it should be turned on in.

It's not wise to turn on the SAP Companion editor within the SAP Companion configuration in Manager, particularly once that configuration is used in a Production system, but as a test to see if it really is Companion, turn on the editor within the configuration in Manager and then refresh Concur.  If it is your configuration, the pencil should appear.

Hope this helps.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: How can we get details like Created By and Created On...
entry.content: We don't expose the author or last change date for example. However if you expose the CDS View as an API you can get the field properties programmatically using the $metadata OData method as I mentioned above. Have a look also at 35D (https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2402/SolP/35D?region=DE). You can also look into extended the data model with Developer Extensibility (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53b14a3c9f7d4837a9c1f271ee3b24a4/e86da62802f54212b2a255efb0224937.html?locale=en-US). But I think this will not help you with the Author or Last Changed info for the CDS View itself. If you describe your end goal/business requirement that you are trying to achieve, we may be able to provide some other guidance.

entry.title: Re: Simulation SAP Enable Now
entry.content: If this option is selected then when you first record a simulation, all Practice bubbles will be positioned in the center of the screen. Authors can still move them; it's just a default. And changing this setting after recording won't magically move them - it's only used at the time of recording (which is why it's in Authoring Settings > Recording...).

entry.title: Re: COGI BAPI
entry.content: You can set up a batch job to run the program CORUPROC / CORUAFWP at defined frequency. For CORUAFWP , you can specify a plant and storage location and save a variant, then set up a batch job with the program and variant (T-code: SM36). For CORUPROC , you can save a variant and set up a batch job (T-code: SM36).

entry.title: Implications of the TLS change on the SAP Linux servers
entry.content:
Does your current version of S4 HANA support TLS 1.2 and are there any changes that need to be done within the application if we change the TLS settings on the Server hosting the S4 HANA application?

If we change the SSL version to support SSL 3.0 and above within the server OS, would there be any changes and impact on the S4 HANA application ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Implications of the TLS change on the SAP Linux servers
entry.content:
Yes, the current version of S4 HANA does support TLS 1.2. TLS is widely used for communication and data transfer over a network. If you change the TLS settings on the server hosting the S4 HANA application, there should not be a significant impact on the application itself.

In term of SSL 3.0, it is largely outdated and prone to certain types of security vulnerabilities. As such, to uphold the highest level of data security, it is strongly recommended to use the latest version of TLS instead of SSL 3.0.

If you decide to configure your server to use SSL 3.0, you might face compatibility issues or possible communication errors with some clients or services which do not support outdated SSL protocol. You would also potentially be placing your data at risk due to the outdated security of SSL 3.0.

entry.title: Re: Getting started with Agile for SAP - 8 steps to success
entry.content:
The success factor for agile is adoption: if you feel you will fail to reach 80/90% adoption rate when moving from waterfall methodology, then you can look at Wagile methodology: https://cloudcoach.com/blog/what-is-wagile-project-management/

These is an intermediate methodology where the business need, personas, use cases is collected in agile mode, while keeping the testing, delivery, documentation in waterfall mode.

these can be a 2-5 years period to convince business people of the value of agile methodology.

entry.title: Re: How to refresh ALV Data in SALV on Click Event.
entry.content:
Hey @Sandra_Rossi Please find my Code Complete below.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Class (Implementation) lcl_data
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CLASS lcl_data IMPLEMENTATION.
METHOD get_data.
SELECT a~belnr,
a~bstat,
b~company_code,
b~fiscal_year,
b~posting_date,
b~document_date,
b~document_type
FROM bkpf AS a
INNER JOIN zfit_cashjournal AS b
ON a~belnr = b~document_number
INTO TABLE @rt_data
WHERE a~bstat = 'V'
AND b~zdoth_tdr_no = @space
AND b~company_code = '1000'.

IF sy-subrc = 0.
SORT rt_data BY belnr.
DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM rt_data COMPARING belnr.
ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

METHOD process_data.
gt_tdrdisp = it_data.
ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form display
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& --> p1 text
*& <-- p2 text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display.
*Create alv object.
TRY.
CALL METHOD cl_salv_table=>factory
IMPORTING
r_salv_table = lo_gr_alv
CHANGING
t_table = gt_tdrdisp.
CATCH cx_salv_msg.
ENDTRY.

lo_gr_alv->set_screen_status( pfstatus = 'ZINTTDRS'
report = sy-repid
set_functions = lo_gr_alv->c_functions_all
).
lr_selections = lo_gr_alv->get_selections( ).
lr_selections->set_selection_mode( if_salv_c_selection_mode=>row_column ).

lo_gr_functions = lo_gr_alv->get_functions( ).
lo_gr_functions->set_all( abap_true ).

* Fit the columns
lo_columns = lo_gr_alv->get_columns( ).
lo_columns->set_optimize( abap_true ).

lo_columns = lo_gr_alv->get_columns( ).

"events
gr_events = lo_gr_alv->get_event( ).
CREATE OBJECT event_handler.
SET HANDLER event_handler->user_command FOR gr_events.

* Display alv
lo_gr_alv->display( ).

ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Class (Implementation) lcl_handel_events
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CLASS lcl_handel_events IMPLEMENTATION.
METHOD user_command.

DATA: gt_final TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zfit_cashjournal.

SELECT *
FROM zfit_cashjournal
INTO TABLE gt_final.

gt_rows = lr_selections->get_selected_rows( ).

CASE e_salv_function.
WHEN 'GTDR'.
IF gt_rows IS INITIAL.
MESSAGE 'Please Select atleast one document' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
ELSE.
TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_docgen,
document_number TYPE zfit_cashjournal-document_number,

company_code TYPE zfit_cashjournal-company_code,
fiscal_year TYPE zfit_cashjournal-fiscal_year,
item TYPE zfit_cashjournal-item,
fund TYPE zfit_cashjournal-fund,
receipt_key TYPE zfit_cashjournal-receipt_key,
doc_num TYPE char5,
END OF ts_docgen.


DATA: lt_docgen TYPE TABLE OF ts_docgen.
LOOP AT gt_rows INTO DATA(gs_rows) .
READ TABLE gt_tdrdisp INTO gs_tdrdisp INDEX gs_rows.
APPEND gs_tdrdisp TO gt_temp.
ENDLOOP.


SELECT document_number,
company_code,
fiscal_year,
item,
fund,
receipt_key
FROM zfit_cashjournal
FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @gt_temp
WHERE document_number = @gt_temp-belnr
INTO TABLE @lt_docgen.

* LOOP AT gt_temp INTO gs_temp.
LOOP AT lt_docgen ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_docgen>).
<fs_docgen>-fund = <fs_docgen>-fund(1).
<fs_docgen>-receipt_key = <fs_docgen>-receipt_key(1).
* APPEND gs_docgen TO gt_final.
ENDLOOP.

DATA(lt_temp_gen) = lt_docgen.
SORT lt_temp_gen BY fund receipt_key.
DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_temp_gen COMPARING fund receipt_key.

DATA: lv_document_number TYPE char5.


LOOP AT lt_temp_gen ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_temp_gen>).
CLEAR : lv_document_number.

CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
EXPORTING
nr_range_nr = '01'
object = 'ZFI_INTTDR'
* QUANTITY = '1'
* SUBOBJECT = ' '
* TOYEAR = '0000'
* IGNORE_BUFFER = ' '
IMPORTING
number = lv_document_number
* QUANTITY =
* RETURNCODE =
EXCEPTIONS
interval_not_found = 1
number_range_not_intern = 2
object_not_found = 3
quantity_is_0 = 4
quantity_is_not_1 = 5
interval_overflow = 6
buffer_overflow = 7
OTHERS = 8.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
<fs_temp_gen>-doc_num = lv_document_number.
ENDIF.

LOOP AT lt_docgen ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_gen>).
IF <fs_gen>-fund(1) = <fs_temp_gen>-fund(1)
AND <fs_gen>-receipt_key(1) = <fs_temp_gen>-receipt_key(1).
<fs_gen>-doc_num = lv_document_number.
ENDIF.

UPDATE zfit_cashjournal SET zdoth_tdr_no = <fs_gen>-doc_num
WHERE document_number = <fs_gen>-document_number
AND item = <fs_gen>-item
AND fiscal_year = <fs_gen>-fiscal_year.
ENDLOOP.
ENDLOOP.
MESSAGE 'Internal TDR Created for the Selected Documents' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'S'.

* BREAK-POINT.
* ENDLOOP.
lo_gr_alv->refresh( refresh_mode = if_salv_c_refresh=>full ).
ENDIF.
WHEN 'BACK'.
LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.
* WHEN 'REFRESH'.
*
* lo_gr_alv->get_selections( ).
* lo_gr_alv->refresh( ).
ENDCASE.
ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Change of valuation category - What is the best approach?
entry.content:
I understand valuation category of materials for which orders/batches exists cannot be changed until the orders and batches are archived and deleted.

I have around 20000 materials in plant 1001. These materials are also present in multiple plants. The requirement is to have "Batch SPLIT valuation" in only plant 1001. The rest of the plants don't need any change. What is the best approach to achieve this?

1. Create new materials for all 20000 materials and extend it to all plants (and create new master data for BOMs, Routings as well)?

OR

2. Create a new plant (Let's say Plant 1002) and extend the existing materials to this new plant and change valuation category to "X" (Batch Split Valuation)?

entry.title: Re: Setting Year parameter to Data Action based on input...

Fecha Inicio Proyección This one can be set using the Input control. make sure the parameter is set as below
